title: Podman REST API - can't set context directory for building an image
body:
/kind bug

podman REST API - can't set context directory for building an image

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. A .tar package containing all files incl. the Dockerfile is created and stored on a remote-repository (e.g. github)
2. Jenkins downloads the .tar file mentioned above to a temporally directory.
3. Jenkins uses the the following curl statement to upload the .tar to the podman "Server" and tries to build a new image:
 `curl -X POST --header Content-Type: application/x-tar --data-binary @/data/work/simpletest/simpleTest.tar -v http://192.168.0.3:2345/v3.0.0/libpod/build?simpleTest.tar `

**Describe the results you received:**
```
expire in 0 ms for 6 (transfer 0x55c7066c1fb0)
  Trying 192.168.0.3...
 TCP_NODELAY set
 Expire in 200 ms for 4 (transfer 0x55c7066c1fb0)
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed

  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0* Connected to 192.168.0.3 (192.168.0.3) port 2345 (#0)
> POST /v3.0.0/libpod/build?simpleTest.tar HTTP/1.1
> Host: 192.168.0.3:2345
> User-Agent: curl/7.64.0
> Accept: */*
> Content-Type: application/x-tar
> Content-Length: 1388
> Expect: 100-continue
> 
 Expire in 1000 ms for 0 (transfer 0x55c7066c1fb0)
< HTTP/1.1 100 Continue
} [1388 bytes data]
 We are completely uploaded and fine
< HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
< Api-Version: 1.40
< Content-Type: application/json
< Libpod-Api-Version: 3.2.3
< Server: Libpod/3.2.3 (linux)
< Date: Wed, 18 Aug 2021 14:13:09 GMT
< Transfer-Encoding: chunked
< 
{ [278 bytes data]

100  1758    0   370  100  1388  16818  63090 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:-- 87900
 Connection #0 to host 192.168.0.3 left intact
{"cause":"stat /var/tmp/libpod_builder331445085/build/Containerfile: no such file or directory","message":"failed to parse query parameter 'dockerfile': \"Dockerfile\": stat /var/tmp/libpod_builder331445085/build/Containerfile: no such file or directory","response":400}
{"error":"stat /var/tmp/libpod_builder331445085/build/Containerfile: no such file or directory\n"}
```

So, podman still assumes the wrong context directory.
I checked the /data/work/simpletest folder and the simpleTest.tar got successfully received. 

**Describe the results you expected:**
Checking the REST-API documentation I couldn't find any hint on how to specify a "working directory" or "Context directory" for the build. Maybe there is one, but then it's undocumented ...


**Output of `podman version`:**


comments.body:
@rhatdan You've been knocking out `podman-remote build` issues - any chance you want to take a shot at this one?

@blindshare It would help greatly if you can get us one of these tar files that Jenkins sends, so we can reproduce ourselves.

> @rhatdan You've been knocking out `podman-remote build` issues - any chance you want to take a shot at this one?
> 
> @blindshare It would help greatly if you can get us one of these tar files that Jenkins sends, so we can reproduce ourselves.

Hi @mheon, you can simply pull the .tar file from my github repo using:
`curl -L https://github.com/blindshare/simpleCherryPyPage/tarball/master > simpleTest.tar`

@blindshare, Using your reproducer and podman built from main, I was successful with the following:

```
curl -X POST --header 'Content-Type: application/x-tar' --data-binary @simpleTest.tar -v \
--unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock \
http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=blindshare-simpleCherryPyPage-64f9421/Dockerfile
```

Please note, I used the `dockerfile` query parameter to point to the `Dockerfile` in the tar file. [ImageBuildLibpod reference](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/ImageBuildLibpod) provides the details on calling our API.

Please feel free to reopen issue if you feel I have missed something.
